title: orobanche flava subsp. cicerbitae
intro: Orobanche flava subsp. cicerbitae, also known as yellow broomrape or Orobanche flava subsp., is a parasitic flowering plant that belongs to the Orobanchaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Orobanche flava subsp. cicerbitae include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made by infusing the roots and leaves in water or oil, and it is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
